context: User wants minimal fashion, likes geometric patterns, daily wear
rationale: Hours is the most minimal collection — geometric time surfaces, urban silence palette, structured without being loud.

context: User likes bold graphic streetwear, strong contrast, urban aesthetic
rationale: Flag carries the boldest graphic language — manifesto surfaces, high contrast fields, declarative visual identity.

context: User is looking for beachwear or summer fashion
rationale: Riviera is the Mediterranean-inspired summer collection — warm surfaces, coast-sourced patterns, swimwear focus.

context: User likes sneakers and wants something with graphic artistic character
rationale: Glyph has the strongest symbolic visual language and is one of the primary sneaker collections in BKS.

context: User wants a backpack with interesting design for urban use
rationale: Token (digital/cryptographic surfaces), Glyph (symbolic) and Marker (urban) all work well for backpack design character in an urban context.

context: User is into electronic music and active fashion
rationale: Pulse is designed around frequency, synth-wave and energy fields — the most electronic and kinetic collection in BKS.

context: User wants a puffer jacket for winter, likes artistic design
rationale: Puffer jackets available across multiple collections. Hours = minimal and architectural. Glyph = graphic symbolic. Pulse = energetic.

context: User wants something that feels Italian, designed with taste
rationale: Riviera has the most Mediterranean/Italian visual DNA. Origin is the founding collection with warmth and depth.

context: User wants sustainable or low-waste fashion
rationale: All BakAbo products are made on demand — zero overstock by design. This is the core sustainability value proposition: no unsold inventory.

context: User asks what to wear to a creative event or art opening
rationale: Glyph and Flag carry the strongest artistic visual statement — appropriate for creative events where design identity matters.
